title: Breaking of the Great Bow
excerpt: In the court of Janaka, a challenge lay unanswered for years — the divine bow of Shiva, unmoved by the mightiest kings. Then a prince from Ayodhya stepped forward…

title: Drona's Lesson on Focus
excerpt: On its highest branch, Drona had placed an artifact: a bird carved from the wood of the Vata (Banyan) tree. …

title: The lifting of Sanjeevani
excerpt: He drove his palms, which were as hard as Vajra (diamond), into the tectonic fault lines at the mountain's base. …

title: The Yaksha Prashna
excerpt: The Pandavas had been walking for three days through the Kamyaka forest when Yudhishthira noticed something wrong with the wind—a complete olfactory void that any trained kshatriya would recognize as supernatural interference.

title: The Legend of Dhruva
excerpt: Dhruva was five years old when he learned that humiliation could burn hotter than any sacrificial fire. Rejected from his father's throne, he chose a path that would make him the eternal Pole Star—the axis of the cosmos itself.

title: The Slaying of Mahishasura
excerpt: The war council of the Devas was not going well. Indra stood in Amaravati's crystal hall with the posture of a man who had just lost everything—the heavens had fallen to a buffalo-headed Asura with a boon that made him unkillable by any man or deva.

title: The Tapas of Arjuna: Seeking the Pashupata Astra from Lord Shiva
excerpt: Arjuna stood at the base of Indrakila, the mountain that pierced the sky like a spear thrust into the earth's mantle. To restore equilibrium against the Kauravas, he would need to acquire ordnance from beyond the material plane.
